evals.prompt: How do I design an onboarding flow using in-app messages for our SaaS product?
evals.expected_output: Progressive onboarding flow with contextual tooltips, modals, and checklists

assertions.name: progressive_disclosure
assertions.description: Response must recommend progressive disclosure — showing steps contextually as users reach features, not all at once

assertions.name: trigger_design
assertions.description: Response must explain event-based triggers (not just time-based) for showing each onboarding step

assertions.name: completion_tracking
assertions.description: Response must mention tracking onboarding completion rates to measure effectiveness

evals.prompt: When should I use in-app messages vs push notifications vs Content Cards?
evals.expected_output: Decision framework for choosing between in-app, push, and content cards

assertions.name: in_app_for_active_users
assertions.description: Response must explain that in-app messages only reach users currently in the app

assertions.name: push_for_re_engagement
assertions.description: Response must recommend push for reaching users outside the app

assertions.name: content_cards_for_persistence
assertions.description: Response must explain Content Cards as persistent content that users can revisit

evals.prompt: How do I set up in-app messages in Braze as part of a Canvas journey?
evals.expected_output: Braze-specific guide to adding in-app messages and Content Cards to Canvas Flow

assertions.name: canvas_integration
assertions.description: Response must reference Canvas Flow as the tool for orchestrating in-app messages with other channels

assertions.name: message_types
assertions.description: Response must mention Braze in-app message types (Modal, Slideup, Fullscreen, HTML Custom)

assertions.name: trigger_options
assertions.description: Response must explain Braze in-app trigger options (session start, custom event, purchase)
